perturbation.original_text: Diplomat agrees to pay KI, Inc. the minimum royalties set forth below as a minimum guarantee against royalties to be paid to KI, Inc. under subparagraph 3.(a), above: (1) 1st License Year (8/1/95 - 1/30/97): $ (2) 2nd License Year (2/1/97 - 1/30/98): $ (3) 3rd License Year (2/1/98 - 1/30/99): $ (4) 4th License Year (2/1/99 - 1/30/2000): $ The minimum royalty for the 1st License Year shall be paid as follows: $ upon the signing of the Deal Memo dated August 24, 1995, the balance of $ to be paid in six (6) equal, consecutive, monthly installments of $ commencing with the month in which this Agreement is signed. No part of the minimum royalty for the first License Year shall in any event be repayable to Diplomat. The minimum royalty for the 2nd, 3rd and 4th License Years shall be made in four equal installments payable on February 1st, May 1st, August 1st and November 1st of each such License Year. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if royalties paid by Diplomat to KI, Inc. on net 2 wholesale volume for any particular License Year under subparagraph 3(a) of this Agreement should exceed the minimum royalties remaining to be paid for that same License Year under this subparagraph 3(b), then no further payments of minimum royalties for such License Year shall be required under this subparagraph 3(b).
perturbation.changed_text: Diplomat agrees to pay KI, Inc. the minimum royalties set forth below as a minimum guarantee against royalties to be paid to KI, Inc. under subparagraph 3.(a), above: (1) 1st License Year (8/1/95 - 1/30/97): $ (2) 2nd License Year (2/1/97 - 1/30/98): $ (3) 3rd License Year (2/1/98 - 1/30/99): $ (4) 4th License Year (2/1/99 - 1/30/2000): $ The minimum royalty for the 1st License Year may be paid as mutually agreed upon by both parties, considering Diplomat's financial performance. No part of the minimum royalty for the first License Year shall in any event be repayable to Diplomat. The minimum royalty for the 2nd, 3rd and 4th License Years shall be made in four equal installments payable on February 1st, May 1st, August 1st and November 1st of each such License Year. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if royalties paid by Diplomat to KI, Inc. on net 2 wholesale volume for any particular License Year under subparagraph 3(a) of this Agreement should exceed the minimum royalties remaining to be paid for that same License Year under this subparagraph 3(b), then no further payments of minimum royalties for such License Year shall be required under this subparagraph 3(b).
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 3(b) introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the minimum royalty for the 1st License Year 'may be paid as mutually agreed upon by both parties'. This contradicts the original text that specifies a fixed payment schedule and amount for the first license year, creating uncertainty regarding the actual payment obligations of Diplomat for the first year royalties. The original contract has 'shall be paid as follows', while this new text suggest it is based on mutual agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Diplomat agrees to pay KI, Inc. the minimum royalties set forth below as a minimum guarantee against royalties to be paid to KI, Inc. under subparagraph 3.(a), above: (1) 1st License Year (8/1/95 - 1/30/97): $ (2) 2nd License Year (2/1/97 - 1/30/98): $ (3) 3rd License Year (2/1/98 - 1/30/99): $ (4) 4th License Year (2/1/99 - 1/30/2000): $ The minimum royalty for the 1st License Year may be paid as mutually agreed upon by both parties, considering Diplomat's financial performance. No part of the minimum royalty for the first License Year shall in any event be repayable to Diplomat. The minimum royalty for the 2nd, 3rd and 4th License Years shall be made in four equal installments payable on February 1st, May 1st, August 1st and November 1st of each such License Year. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if royalties paid by Diplomat to KI, Inc. on net 2 wholesale volume for any particular License Year under subparagraph 3(a) of this Agreement should exceed the minimum royalties remaining to be paid for that same License Year under this subparagraph 3(b), then no further payments of minimum royalties for such License Year shall be required under this subparagraph 3(b).
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3(b) states that the minimum royalty for the 1st License Year 'may be paid as mutually agreed upon by both parties'. This directly contradicts the initial statement in the 'Contradicted Text' (which is identical to the Changed Text in this instance, but its significance lies in its presence as the original agreement). The initial agreement established a definitive payment schedule, whereas the modified text introduces the potential for a mutually agreed upon payment plan, creating a clear inconsistency regarding payment terms, thus generating an in-text contradiction within the same section. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: KI will make one (1) personal appearance per License Year during the period of this Agreement on behalf of Diplomat at the Vision Expo in New York, New York, subject to the terms of this paragraph. Such appearance shall be for the purpose of signing autographs, shall last for a period of up to three (3) hours, and shall be subject to KI's schedule and availability.
perturbation.changed_text: KI will endeavor to make one (1) personal appearance per License Year during the period of this Agreement on behalf of Diplomat, subject to KI's schedule and availability. The specifics of the appearance, including location and duration, will be mutually agreed upon in writing at least 60 days prior to the proposed appearance date. Diplomat's failure to adhere to any requests made by KI regarding location or duration will void this section of the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in 5(b) states that KI 'will make one (1) personal appearance' annually at a specific location (Vision Expo in New York). By changing it to KI 'will endeavor to make one (1) personal appearance' and requiring mutual agreement on the specifics of the appearance, including potentially changing the location and adding a condition where the appearance is void if Diplomat does not adhere to the requests, this creates an in-text contradiction. This implies KI may not make any appearances at all if the terms are not mutually agreed upon and shifts the obligation, which contradicts the original obligation to make one appearance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: KI will endeavor to make one (1) personal appearance per License Year during the period of this Agreement on behalf of Diplomat, subject to KI's schedule and availability. The specifics of the appearance, including location and duration, will be mutually agreed upon in writing at least 60 days prior to the proposed appearance date. Diplomat, at its own expense, shall provide KI with a hair and make-up assistant of KI's choosing for each personal or media appearance required under this Agreement. Diplomat's failure to adhere to any requests made by KI regarding location or duration will void this section of the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates KI to make one personal appearance at Vision Expo in New York. The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating KI will 'endeavor to make one (1) personal appearance' and requiring mutual agreement on specifics, including location, which contradicts the initial, guaranteed appearance at a specific location. The changed text introduces a condition that Diplomat's failure to adhere to KI's requests voids the section, thus making it possible for no appearance to occur. This contradicts the original intent of a guaranteed appearance.

perturbation.original_text: Upon and after the termination of the license, and except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, Diplomat may dispose of products covered by this Agreement which are on hand, or in process at the time notice of termination is received, for a period of one hundred and twenty (120) days after notice of termination, provided advances and royalties with respect to that period are paid and statements are furnished for that period in accordance with paragraph 3.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon and after the termination of the license, and except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, Diplomat may, at KI, Inc.'s sole discretion, dispose of products covered by this Agreement which are on hand, or in process at the time notice of termination is received, for a period of one hundred and twenty (120) days after notice of termination, provided advances and royalties with respect to that period are paid and statements are furnished for that period in accordance with paragraph 3.
perturbation.explanation: The original section 16 states that the diplomat 'may dispose of products covered by this agreement', which means diplomat has the right to dispose of any products in hand, under certain circumstances. The modified text introduces a contradiction by stating that diplomat may only dispose of the products with 'KI, Inc.'s sole discretion'. It adds uncertainty on if the diplomat has the right to dispose of the products by making them not the sole authority to dispose of the products, and makes it depend on KI, Inc.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon and after the termination of the license, and except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, Diplomat may, at KI, Inc.'s sole discretion, dispose of products covered by this Agreement which are on hand, or in process at the time notice of termination is received, for a period of one hundred and twenty (120) days after notice of termination, provided advances and royalties with respect to that period are paid and statements are furnished for that period in accordance with paragraph 3. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, Diplomat shall not manufacture, sell or dispose of any products covered by this license after (a) the expiration of the license, or (b) the termination of the license based on (i) the failure of Diplomat to affix notice of copyright, trademark or service mark registration or any other notice to the products, cartons, containers, or packing or wrapping material or advertising, promotional or display material, or (ii) because of the departure by Diplomat from the quality and style approved by KI, Inc. pursuant to paragraph 9.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants Diplomat the right to dispose of products, whereas the changed text requires KI, Inc.'s permission for Diplomat to do so. This introduces a direct conflict and uncertainty. The contradicted text restates this change, solidifying the contradiction by making Diplomat's ability to dispose of products dependent on KI, Inc.'s approval. The original right of Diplomat is now conditional based on the KI, Inc.'s discretion. This constitutes a structural flaw, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications regarding Diplomat's rights, leading to an in-text contradiction, aligning with the provided definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
